title: T-008 — API: Items list (GET /api/technical/items) with filters
prompt: Implement Next.js App Router route GET /api/technical/items per §6.2. Accept query: type (csv item_types), status, allergens (csv codes — match items having those allergens via item_allergen_profiles), d365_sync_status, q (item_code/name search). Return paginated array (default limit 50). Use Drizzle. Apply RLS via session-level SET app.current_org_id from auth context. Zod validate query parameters; include validation messages for V-TEC-01 codes match per type pattern (informational). RED in tests/api/items-list.test.ts using msw or supertest pattern. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/api/items-list.test.ts`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Items list endpoint with filters, pagination, RLS-aware SELECT.
pipeline_inputs.details: Use Server Action variant via app/api/technical/items/route.ts. Pagination via cursor or offset+limit (consistent with project convention). Filter by allergens uses EXISTS subquery joining item_allergen_profiles. Response shape: { items: ItemDTO[], total, next_cursor }. ItemDTO maps SQL columns to camelCase.
acceptance_criteria: Given org A and org B both have items, when GET /api/technical/items is called as org A, then only org A items are returned (RLS).
Given query type=fg&status=active, when records exist, then only fg+active items are returned.
Given query allergens=A01, when an item profile has A01, then it appears; otherwise it does not.
Given missing/invalid auth, when GET runs, then 401 is returned.
test_strategy: RED test asserting RLS isolation + filter combos.
risk_red_lines: Do not return private_jsonb to non-owner roles.
Do not bypass RLS via service-role connection.
